question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: <cite index="11-1,11-4">The American Staffing Association projects Q1 2026 GDP growth at 2.1%</cite>, which exceeds the 2.0% threshold. <cite index="19-4">Goldman Sachs forecasts particularly strong Q1 2026 growth at 3.5% (annualized quarterly rate)</cite>, while <cite index="12-8">the NY Fed DSGE model indicates Q1 2026 growth is more than one percentage point higher than previously predicted</cite>. <cite index="19-19,19-20">Multiple forecasters expect 2026 annual growth around 2.6%, with Q1 getting a mechanical boost from the government shutdown recovery</cite>. The consensus among professional forecasters appears to support growth above 2.0% for Q1 2026, suggesting the market is undervaluing this probability.
key_factors: Multiple forecasters project Q1 2026 GDP growth above 2.1%
Mechanical boost from government shutdown recovery expected in Q1
Goldman Sachs forecasts 3.5% annualized growth for Q1 2026
Consensus annual 2026 growth forecasts of 2.2-2.8% support strong Q1
risks: Economic forecasts historically have significant margin of error
Q1 data could be affected by seasonal adjustments and revisions
Government shutdown effects may be less positive than anticipated
Consumer spending uncertainty could weigh on growth
BEA advance estimate methodology may differ from forecasts
